title: townsendia alpigena var. minima
intro: Townsendia alpigena var. minima, also known as Western alpine Townsend daisy, is a flowering plant species belonging to the daisy family Asteraceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Townsendia alpigena var. minima include its potential to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial activities. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
